reference_audit.notes: Tests use only in-scope RFC 9002 IDs. Source helpers carry no requirement IDs, matching the repository convention for this slice.

requirements.title: Separate loss detection by packet number space
requirements.remaining_gap: Needs packet-number-space-specific loss-state tracking to prove isolation beyond helper-level timing formulas.
requirements.notes: No sender/recovery state exists for this clause in the current helper-focused slice.

requirements.title: Declare loss only for packets that satisfy the basic loss criteria
requirements.notes: Static helper covers the basic preconditions for declaring a packet lost.

requirements.title: Allow smaller initial reordering thresholds with adaptation
requirements.remaining_gap: Needs an adaptive reordering-threshold policy; the current slice only exposes static loss timing math.
requirements.notes: No sender/recovery state exists for this clause in the current helper-focused slice.

requirements.title: Recommend a packet threshold of three
requirements.notes: The helper exposes the recommended packet-threshold constant of 3.

requirements.title: Avoid packet thresholds below three
requirements.notes: The helper rejects packet thresholds below 3.

requirements.title: Declare earlier packets lost after sufficient time
requirements.notes: Remaining time is computed from the packet send time and the RFC 9002 time threshold.

requirements.title: Bound the time threshold by timer granularity
requirements.notes: The loss delay is bounded by the local timer granularity.

requirements.title: Compute the time threshold from RTT and granularity
requirements.notes: The loss delay uses max(latest_rtt, smoothed_rtt) scaled by kTimeThreshold.

requirements.title: Schedule a timer for the remaining time before declaring loss
requirements.notes: The helper reports the remaining time before the loss deadline fires.

requirements.title: Use the recommended packet-threshold multiplier
requirements.notes: Default time-threshold multiplier is 9/8.

requirements.title: Use a one-millisecond timer granularity
requirements.notes: Default timer granularity is 1 millisecond.

requirements.title: Allow alternative time-threshold experiments
requirements.remaining_gap: Needs sender/recovery state that is not present in the helper-only slice.
requirements.notes: No sender/recovery state exists for this clause in the current helper-focused slice.

requirements.title: Send probe datagrams on PTO expiration or address-validation uncertainty
requirements.remaining_gap: Needs a sender/transmit surface that can emit probe datagrams and apply address-validation gating.
requirements.notes: No sender/recovery state exists for this clause in the current helper-focused slice.

requirements.title: Compute PTO per packet number space
requirements.notes: PTO is computed per packet number space and the helper takes the space explicitly.

requirements.title: Do not infer loss from PTO expiration
requirements.remaining_gap: Needs sender-side loss-recovery orchestration to prove PTO expiration does not itself mark packets lost.
requirements.notes: No sender/recovery state exists for this clause in the current helper-focused slice.

requirements.title: Schedule PTO after ack-eliciting transmission
requirements.notes: The helper implements PTO = smoothed_rtt + max(4*rttvar, kGranularity) + max_ack_delay.

requirements.title: Set max_ack_delay to zero for early handshake spaces
requirements.notes: Initial and Handshake spaces force max_ack_delay to zero in the PTO formula.

requirements.title: Keep PTO above granularity
requirements.notes: The PTO helper floors the result at the local timer granularity.

requirements.title: Use the earlier PTO across Initial and Handshake spaces
requirements.notes: A helper selects the earlier PTO between Initial and Handshake spaces.

requirements.title: Defer application-data PTO until handshake confirmation
requirements.notes: Application Data PTO is withheld until handshake confirmation is present.

requirements.title: Restart PTO on send, acknowledgment, or key discard
requirements.remaining_gap: Needs sender/recovery state transitions to restart PTO after sends, acknowledgments, or key discard.
requirements.notes: No sender/recovery state exists for this clause in the current helper-focused slice.

requirements.title: Increase PTO backoff on timeout
requirements.notes: The backoff helper doubles the PTO period once per timeout.

requirements.title: Reset PTO backoff on acknowledgment
requirements.remaining_gap: Needs sender/recovery state transitions to reset PTO backoff on acknowledgment.
requirements.notes: No sender/recovery state exists for this clause in the current helper-focused slice.

requirements.title: Suppress PTO-backoff reset on unvalidated Initial acknowledgments
requirements.remaining_gap: Needs handshake- and validation-aware ack-processing state to suppress Initial backoff resets correctly.
requirements.notes: No sender/recovery state exists for this clause in the current helper-focused slice.

requirements.title: Avoid conflicting timers
requirements.notes: The timer selector prefers the loss-detection deadline over PTO when both are available.

requirements.title: Reuse prior-smoothed RTT on resumed connections
requirements.notes: The RTT estimator already accepts a caller-supplied initial RTT and seeds its state from that value.

requirements.title: Default initial RTT to 333 milliseconds
requirements.notes: The default RTT seed remains 333 milliseconds.

requirements.title: Use PATH_CHALLENGE and PATH_RESPONSE timing for initial RTT
requirements.notes: PATH_CHALLENGE/PATH_RESPONSE timing is exposed as a standalone elapsed-time helper.

requirements.title: Do not treat PATH_CHALLENGE/PATH_RESPONSE delay as an RTT sample
requirements.notes: The path-validation timing helper does not mutate RTT state; callers can feed the elapsed time into a seed value separately.

requirements.title: Reset timers when keys are discarded
requirements.remaining_gap: Needs key-discard plumbing that resets the PTO and loss-detection timers.
requirements.notes: No sender/recovery state exists for this clause in the current helper-focused slice.

requirements.title: Delay server PTO until address validation traffic arrives
requirements.remaining_gap: Needs client/server PTO-arming logic and address-validation state.
requirements.notes: No sender/recovery state exists for this clause in the current helper-focused slice.

requirements.title: Reset the server PTO when the client sends data
requirements.remaining_gap: Needs client/server PTO-arming logic and address-validation state.
